defs: The act of mocking, deriding, and exposing to contempt, by mimicry, by insincere imitation, or by a false show of earnestness; a counterfeit appearance.
quotes: It is, as the air, invulnerable, And our vain blows malicious mockery.
Grace at meals is now generally so performed as to look more like a mockery upon devotion than any solemn application of the mind to God.
And bear about the mockery of woe.

defs: Insulting or contemptuous action or speech; contemptuous merriment; derision; ridicule.
Subject of laughter, derision, or sport.
quotes: The cruel handling of the city whereof they made a mockery.
